turns.user_message: I woke up with this weird tightness across my chest, like a heavy band just wrapped around my ribs. It only started a few days ago, but it’s already making it hard to focus on anything. Every time I sit down to tackle my usual list, my shoulders automatically hike up toward my ears. I keep trying to roll them back and take deep breaths, but the knot just stays right there. It’s honestly just a really rough stretch of days, and my body seems completely out of sync with what I need to do.

That heavy pressure in my chest hasn't gone away at all, and now it feels like a permanent fixture in my daily routine. I catch myself holding my breath every single time I look at the next thing on my schedule. My neck and back are constantly stiff, and rubbing them doesn't do a thing to loosen the tension. I used to be able to shake off a rough morning, but lately I just carry this tightness straight through until I finally step away. It’s exhausting to feel so physically wound up over things I should be able to handle.

My whole body feels like a tightly wound spring that is just waiting to snap, and I can barely function through the day anymore. That familiar tightness in my ribs has spread everywhere, making my hands actually shake when I try to start anything. I sit there staring at the same screen for hours while my muscles lock up completely. Every single task feels like it requires a physical effort I just don't have left in me. I feel completely drained and trapped inside my own skin, with zero energy to push through the mounting pressure.

generation_metadata.diversity_anchor: The speaker opens by describing a physical feeling. This just started recently.
